metric_name: IBM decision to replace Karat with Tivoli
metric_value: IBM chose to replace Karat project with Tivoli rather than augment Karat; positioned SystemView and NetView as Tivoli management applications
Karat would have been delivered years after Tivoli; would manage only IBM platforms; production-critical app suppliers were already on Tivoli, not Karat
notes: IBM's internal rationale for Tivoli acquisition

metric_value: TMF manages 18+ platform operating environments including Unix variants, Windows NT, NetWare, MVS, OS/400, OS/2, DOS
TMF framework allows plug-in of best-in-class ISV management applications; 45+ ISV partners in 10/Plus association
HP OpenView provides bottoms-up view of enterprise; Tivoli provides top-down view based on IT management policies/procedures

metric_name: CA Unicenter TNG vs Tivoli comparison
metric_value: CA Unicenter TNG is comprehensive but must be purchased in entirety; less flexible for integrating other management applications

metric_name: Tivoli success as IBM enterprise management strategy
metric_value: IBM has done an about-face and charged ahead with Tivoli; business-line executives, IT executives and IS managers will find their needs realized
IBM Tivoli became a multi-billion dollar franchise; Tivoli brand active 20+ years after acquisition; IBM Tivoli Monitoring still referenced in IBM bulletins as of 2024-2026
notes: IBM Monitoring Academy Newsletter July-August 2024; IBM security bulletins 2024-2026 reference active Tivoli products

metric_value: IBM's acquisition provides global consulting and support team for Tivoli-based solutions; validates ISV and user investments
IBM built extensive Tivoli professional services organization; Tivoli products became core to IBM Global Services; ISV ecosystem grew substantially
notes: Consistent with IBM's documented Tivoli investment through 2000s

metric_name: TME 10 Enterprise Console centralized management capability
metric_value: TME 10 EC removes administrators from details of underlying technology; manages according to enterprise policy; provides integrated unified view of IT resources

metric_name: TMF benefit 1: Deploy without holding back
metric_value: Enables rapid deployment of client-server applications instead of holding back due to management cost concerns
source_page: Executive Summary / Managing Network Computing section

metric_value: Extends reach of centralized resources throughout enterprise; reduces cost of administration
source_page: Executive Summary / Managing Network Computing section

metric_value: Unifies management of applications, operating environments, platforms and networks from mainframe to desktop
source_page: Executive Summary / Managing Network Computing section

metric_value: Manages evolving IT environment using policies and procedures instead of administrative skill or whim
source_page: Executive Summary / Managing Network Computing section

metric_value: IT executives have found managing client-server environments to be more costly than anticipated; standardizing on fewest application platforms possible

metric_name: IBM Karat vs Tivoli user validation
metric_value: IBM's largest users told IBM they were already deploying the real Karat — and it was Tivoli; validates IBM's acquisition rationale
notes: Critical strategic insight from user research

metric_value: TMF employs managers and agents as smart objects carrying out enterprise policies/procedures; well designed to scale as IT environment grows
Controls distribution of software patches, updates, upgrades from central location to any managed Tivoli system (from OS/390 to desktops)
Monitors conditions on managed resources; provides alerts; enables thresholds for wide variety of conditions; takes automatic corrective actions

metric_name: IBM SystemView repositioning as Tivoli application
metric_value: IBM's SystemView positioned to become well-behaved Tivoli management application; validates existing SystemView user investments

metric_name: IBM NetView repositioning as Tivoli application
metric_value: IBM's NetView positioned to become well-behaved Tivoli management application; validates existing NetView user investments

metric_name: Tivoli ISV ecosystem sustainability post-IBM acquisition
metric_value: Tivoli has minds and hearts of ISVs, system platform suppliers, packaged application suppliers; where ISVs go, so goes the enterprise
IBM Tivoli grew extensive ISV/partner ecosystem; partners like BMC, CA, and Remedy continued integrating with Tivoli for years; 10/Plus ecosystem expanded significantly
notes: BMC Software still active as major IT management vendor 2026; confirms ongoing ecosystem

metric_value: CA users possess ability to visually integrate networked resources using pre-packaged solution vs Tivoli's ability to functionally integrate with best-in-class solutions

metric_name: Tivoli OS/400 and OS/390 management addition
metric_value: Tivoli will add capability for IS administrators to manage OS/400 and OS/390 (MVS) production environments using TMF

IS management deploys management tools embedded with applications/platforms; many different tools per environment; managing IT infrastructure is fragmented; significant portion of IT budget spent managing infrastructure
Remote branch sites provided same consistent policies and procedures as corporate sites without bloated staff expense
